page: Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs, utilizing the AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the c7a.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance profiles. It achieves top-tier results in LLM inference speed for prompt processing and text generation using small 135M models. CPU-bound benchmarks, including stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests, PassMark CPU scores, and Geekbench multi-core evaluations, consistently place in the top 25%. Memory performance is characterized by strong write bandwidth for large blocks and high read bandwidth for cached small blocks, though uncached large-block read bandwidth falls into the average category.

Given its high-density compute architecture and DDR5 memory, the c7a.8xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for CPU-intensive tasks such as software compilation, ray tracing, database operations, and small-scale LLM inference. However, workloads requiring massive memory footprints or high-capacity local storage may experience tradeoffs, requiring external storage attachments.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor and powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. Featuring 32 dedicated physical cores, 32 vCPUs, and 64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, this x86_64 instance delivers a balanced 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in small-model LLM inference and strong results in multi-core CPU processing, software compilation, and database operations. The c7a.8xlarge is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads, making it an excellent fit for software development, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving, though workloads requiring local NVMe storage or massive memory arrays will face resource tradeoffs.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro virtualization, delivering top-tier performance for CPU-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, optimized for high-performance compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated thirty-two core AMD EPYC compute instance with high-speed DDR5 memory optimized for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores
Equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier performance in small-model LLM inference and text generation
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and ray tracing workloads
